english: Mil.; sgós-su (sgos su), or sgos (sgos) adv., (opp. to spyir (spyir)) particularly, especially; sgos-(kyi) (sgos (kyi)), dpon (dpon) a subaltern officer Cs.; sgós-pa (sgos pa) Sch.: 'to choose, to find the right thing'.
bag, purse; sgyig-gu čád-poi dbaṅ-du sóṅ-nas (sgyig gu chad pa'i dbang du song nas) our purse being at low ebb; dṅul-sgyig (dngul sgyig) money-bag, purse.

pf. bsgyiṅs (bsgyings), fut. bsgyiṅ (bsgying), 1. acc. to Lexx. = Ssk. जृम्भ syn. to glál-ba (glal ba), to yawn, gape, and perh. to stretch one's self after having slept; it is almost exclusively used in describing the attitude of a dying lion, and so also the dying attitude of Buddha. -- 2. perh. also = gyíṅ-ba (gying ba).

1. the hollow of the knee, bend of the knee; knee-joint; sgyid-pa gčód-pa (sgyid pa gcod pa) to lame the knee-joint, to hamstring (a horse) Glr. -- 2. the calf (of the leg) Mil.; sgyid skyúr-ba (sgyid skyur ba) acute pain in the knee and leg e.g. of a woman with child Med.; Cs.: 'to despair'? -- sgyid-kúṅ (sgyid khung) the hollow of the knee Med. -- sgyid-kyól (sgyid kyol) one lame in his legs Cs. -- sgyid-lug-pa (sgyid lug pa) Lex. w. e., Cs.: slothful, idle, lazy; sgyid-lhód (sgyid lhod) Sch. id.
also sgyéd-bu (sgyed bu), a hearth, fire-place, consisting of (three) stones on which the kettle is placed; lčags-sgyid (lcags sgyid) iron trevet, tripod, cf. sgyéd-po (sgyed po).

artifice, imposture Dzl. and elsewh., yyo-sgyú (g.yo sgyu) id.; yyo-sgyu-med-na (g.yo sgyu med na) if he is without guile Dzl.; sgyu-čàn (sgyu can) artful, crafty, cunning, Cs. -- sgyu-prúl-ma (sgyu phrul ma), माया, the name of Buddha's mother. -- sgyú-ma (sgyu ma), माया, illusion, false show, deception of sight, opp. to dṅos (dngos) reality; sgyu-ma sprul-ba (sgyu ma sprul ba) to exhibit a false show Cs.; ṅas snáṅ-ba tams-čád sgyú-mar śes (ngas snang ba thams cad sgyu mar shes) I know that every thing visible, the whole external world, is only an illusion Mil.; sgyú-mai nor (sgyu ma'i nor) apparent riches, hence riches in general Mil. (cf. sgyu-lús (sgyu lus)); sgyú-ma-mkán (sgyu ma mkhan) a juggler Mil.; sgyú-ma-mkán-gyi mčán-bu (sgyu ma mkhan gyi mchan bu), sgyu-mai mčán-bu (sgyu ma'i mchan bu) a juggler's apprentice Lexx. -- sgyu-rtsál (sgyu rtsal) art, skill, dexterity, frq., the Indians, and so also the Tibetans counting 64 arts (or 60 in a round number) Tar. 21, 2. -- sgyu-zóg (sgyu zog) deception, hypocrisy Pth. -- sgyu-lús (sgyu lus) 1. the immaterial, subtile and pure body of the soul in the Bardo, hell etc., hence = yid-kyi lus (yid kyi lus) Thgr. 2. the animal and human body in general, in as much as it is only an apparent body, a phantom, when considered from a higher philosophical point of view Mil.
mother-in-law Stg.; mna-sgyúg (mna' sgyug) both daughter-in-law and mother-in-law.

pf. and fut. bsgyur (bsgyur), (vb. a. to gyúr-ba (gyur ba)) 1. to transform, lus o dod-dgúr (lus 'dod dgur) to transform one's body (i.e. one's self) at pleasure, (Dzl. oS ('os) lus (lus) is to be supplied, or gyúr-te (gyur te) to be read); to transform the royal prerogative into a religious one, v. Krims (khrims). -- 2. to change (colour, one's mind), to alter (something written), hence to correct, to revise. -- 3. to give up, leave off (customs, scruples, doubts, timidity) Glr., pyi-rol-pai čós-lugs (phyi rol pa'i chos lugs) the non-Buddhist religion. -- 4. to turn off or aside (the course of a river); to dissuade, divert, las (las) from Dzl. -- 5. to turn, *jīṅ pa gyúr-ce* (jing pa gyur ce) W. to turn round on one's heel; *jiṅ-pa gyúr-te ltá-če* (jing pa gyur te lta ce) W. to look back; kór-lo sgyúr-ba ('khor lo sgyur ba) to turn a wheel = skór-ba (skor ba); skad sgyúr-ba (skad sgyur ba) to vary, to modulate the voice, also to hum a tune, to sing or whistle, as birds do. -- 6. to govern, rtai k'a sráb-kyis (rta'i kha srab kyis), a horse's mouth by the bridle; also fig. *gyál-po k'a lón-gyi gyur, kyo k'a čúṅ-mẹ gyur* (rgyal po kha blon gyis gyur, khyo kha chung mas gyur) C. the king is governed by his minister, the husband by his wife; o dod-čágs ṅán-pas k'a-sgyur ('dod chags ngan pas kha sgyur) he is governed by evil passions Mil.; k'á-lo sgyúr-ba (kha lo sgyur ba) to govern, prop. and fig., v. k'á-lo (kha lo); śiṅ-rta sgyúr-ba (shing rta sgyur ba) to drive a carriage; in a similar sense dbaṅ sgyúr-ba (dbang sgyur ba) c. la, to have command or control of, to command, dominate, frq.; prob. also to possess Mil. -- 7. to translate, sgra sgyúr-ba (sgra sgyur ba) id. -- 8. to multiply Wdk. (cf. gyúr-ba (gyur ba) 4, and lóg-pa (log pa)); bsgyúr-bya (bsgyur bya) the multiplicand Wdk. -- 9. Lad., Pur. to kill, to slaughter. -- 10. to publish, proclaim, announce *ka-sál gyúr-če* (ka sal gyur ce) W. to pub-
